shellOwnership.runtimeRole: Renders logo, global nav links, user menu, and theme toggle
evidence: src/layouts/AppShell.tsx:14-38 renders <header> with fixed position

shellOwnership.runtimeRole: Primary workspace navigation and section switching
shellOwnership.notes: Two components both render the left rail depending on route. Sidebar.tsx is used on dashboard routes, CollapsibleNav.tsx on workspace routes. No shared state owner.
evidence: src/layouts/Sidebar.tsx imported by src/routes/dashboard.tsx
src/layouts/CollapsibleNav.tsx imported by src/routes/workspace.tsx
captures/shell-light.png shows different rail widths between dashboard and workspace

shellOwnership.runtimeRole: Wraps page content with consistent padding and scroll container
evidence: src/layouts/PageFrame.tsx wraps <Outlet /> in all route layouts
src/styles/tokens.css defines --color-bg-primary, --color-text-primary, etc. with :root and [data-theme=dark] blocks

tokenInventory.driftNotes: Defines a parallel color scale with raw hex values. Some names overlap with CSS tokens but values differ. Dark mode not covered.
evidence: src/theme/colors.ts exports palette object with 'primary', 'secondary' keys using raw #hex
src/theme/colors.ts 'primary' (#2563eb) differs from --color-primary in tokens.css (#3b82f6)

tokenInventory.driftNotes: Extends default colors with project-specific values that do not reference either the CSS tokens or the TypeScript theme.
evidence: tailwind.config.ts extends colors with 'brand-blue': '#1e40af' which matches neither token source

componentContracts.usageNotes: DataTable is the most-imported (8 pages), but InlineTable is used in 3 dashboard pages with a different column API. SettingsGrid is a one-off.
evidence: grep -r 'DataTable' shows 8 imports across src/pages/
grep -r 'InlineTable' shows 3 imports in src/pages/dashboard/
src/pages/settings/SettingsGrid.tsx is not imported outside settings

componentContracts.usageNotes: Single implementation, consistent usage across all pages.
evidence: grep -r 'PageHeader' shows import in all 14 page files

pagePatterns.structureNotes: ProjectsListPage uses PageFrame + DataTable + filter toolbar. DashboardListPage uses its own InlineTable with a custom filter bar.
pagePatterns.shellAlignmentNotes: ProjectsListPage aligns with AppShell. DashboardListPage bypasses PageFrame and manages its own padding.
evidence: src/pages/ProjectsListPage.tsx imports PageFrame and DataTable
src/pages/dashboard/DashboardListPage.tsx does not import PageFrame

pagePatterns.structureNotes: Tabs + content area with consistent layout. Only one detail workspace pattern exists.
evidence: src/pages/ProjectDetailPage.tsx uses PageFrame and TabsContainer

navigationStructure.routeMapping: src/router.tsx defines route tree with layout wrappers per section
navigationStructure.breadcrumbConventions: No breadcrumbs. Pages use PageHeader with a back-link when nested.
navigationStructure.actionPlacement: Primary actions in PageHeader right slot. Table row actions in a dropdown menu.
evidence: src/router.tsx groups routes under DashboardLayout and WorkspaceLayout
No <Breadcrumb> component found in codebase

statePresentation.loading: Inconsistent. Some pages use a shared Spinner component, others use skeleton loaders, two pages use inline 'Loading...' text.
statePresentation.empty: Most pages use an EmptyState component. Two dashboard pages render custom empty markup.
statePresentation.error: ErrorBoundary at the shell level. Page-level error handling varies: some use toast, some use inline alerts, some have no handling.
statePresentation.success: Toast-based via a shared useToast hook. Consistent.
statePresentation.permission: No shared pattern. Two pages check permissions inline and render 'Access denied' text.
statePresentation.async: Long-running operations use polling with no shared convention. Three different polling intervals observed.
evidence: grep -r 'Spinner' shows 6 imports; grep -r 'Skeleton' shows 4 imports; 'Loading...' literal found in 2 files
src/components/ui/EmptyState.tsx imported in 10 pages; custom empty markup in src/pages/dashboard/
src/hooks/useToast.ts imported consistently for success messages

accessibilityNotes: Focus ring visible on interactive elements via Tailwind ring utilities.
Left rail does not trap focus when open on mobile. Escape key does not close it.
DataTable has no aria-label or caption. Row selection is mouse-only.
Dark mode contrast ratios not verified. Some muted-text classes appear low-contrast in captures.

competingImplementations.name: Sidebar
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Static navigation for dashboard routes with fixed width and icon+label links

competingImplementations.name: CollapsibleNav
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Collapsible navigation for workspace routes with expand/collapse state and nested sections

evidence: src/layouts/Sidebar.tsx imported by src/routes/dashboard.tsx
src/layouts/CollapsibleNav.tsx imported by src/routes/workspace.tsx
captures/shell-light.png shows different rail widths: 240px on dashboard, 64px collapsed on workspace
No shared nav state store or consistent width token
conflictBundles.whyNoSingleContract: The two components were built at different times for different sections. Neither was designed to handle the other's use case. No architectural decision was made to unify them.
conflictBundles.recommendedDirection: Unify into a single rail component that supports both static and collapsible modes. CollapsibleNav is the stronger candidate because it already handles the more complex case. The static Sidebar behavior can be expressed as CollapsibleNav with collapse disabled.
discussionQuestions: Should the rail always be collapsible, or should dashboard routes keep a fixed rail?
Should the collapse state persist across navigation or reset per section?
Is the 240px/64px width split intentional, or should both modes share a width token?

conflictBundles.roleUnderDispute: Canonical source of color and theme tokens

competingImplementations.name: CSS custom properties
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Provides semantic color tokens with full light/dark coverage via CSS variables

competingImplementations.name: TypeScript theme object
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Provides a palette object for components that consume theme via JS rather than CSS

competingImplementations.name: Tailwind config extensions
competingImplementations.whatItSolves: Extends Tailwind's default palette with project-specific utility classes

evidence: src/styles/tokens.css --color-primary is #3b82f6
src/theme/colors.ts primary is #2563eb
tailwind.config.ts brand-blue is #1e40af
Three different 'primary blue' values across three sources
conflictBundles.whyNoSingleContract: CSS tokens were the original system. The TypeScript theme was added for a charting library that needed JS values. Tailwind extensions were added ad hoc. No reconciliation was done.
conflictBundles.recommendedDirection: Make CSS custom properties the single source of truth. The TypeScript theme should read from CSS variables at runtime or be generated from the same source. Tailwind config should reference CSS variables via var() rather than defining independent values.
discussionQuestions: Is the charting library's need for JS values still active, or can it consume CSS variables?
Should Tailwind extensions be removed entirely in favor of utility classes that reference CSS tokens?
Are the three different 'primary blue' values intentional (different contexts) or accidental drift?

cleanAreas.summary: Single PageHeader component used consistently across all 14 pages. No competing implementations. Clear ownership in src/components/shell/PageHeader.tsx.
evidence: grep -r 'PageHeader' shows import in all 14 page files
No other page-header-like components found in codebase

cleanAreas.summary: All success feedback uses the shared useToast hook. No competing patterns.
evidence: src/hooks/useToast.ts is the only success notification mechanism
grep -r 'toast' shows consistent usage across mutation handlers
